brokers.name: X-Mode / Outlogic
brokers.incident: Sold MAID+GPS of 400M+ devices including Muslim prayer app users to US military. Banned from app stores Dec 2020.

brokers.name: Cuebiq
brokers.incident: Data sold to hedge funds for consumer foot-traffic investment analysis. Powered The Weather Channel FTC complaint.

brokers.name: SafeGraph
brokers.incident: Sold location data showing visits to abortion clinics, places of worship, and protest sites. Removed from Google Cloud Marketplace Jun 2021.

brokers.name: Veraset
brokers.incident: Sold location data to CDC for COVID-19 mobility surveillance without individual consent. Law enforcement access via commercial contracts, bypassing warrants.

brokers.name: Kochava
brokers.incident: FTC lawsuit Aug 2022 for selling MAID-linked data exposing visits to reproductive health clinics, addiction centers, and military installations.

brokers.name: Placer.ai
brokers.incident: Received Life360 MAID+GPS data to provide retail foot-traffic analytics, including data from minors.

brokers.name: Foursquare (data division)
brokers.incident: Pilgrim SDK harvests location from 40M+ monthly devices. Foot-traffic data sold to financial firms for investment signals.

brokers.name: Near Intelligence
brokers.incident: Claims data on 1.6B people across 44 countries. Sells to both commercial and government clients globally.

brokers.name: Babel Street
brokers.incident: Sells 'Locate X' product — real-time MAID tracking for law enforcement. Used to track visitors to abortion clinics in restrictive US states. Secret Service purchased access.

brokers.name: Venntel (Subsidiary of Gravy Analytics)
brokers.incident: Sold MAID location data to DHS/ICE for immigration enforcement, bypassing warrant requirement. $2M annual contract with DHS documented via FOIA.

brokers.name: Acxiom
brokers.incident: One of the oldest and largest data brokers. Links MAID to offline identity records across 2.5B people globally.

brokers.name: LexisNexis Risk Solutions
brokers.incident: Sells MAID-linked identity resolution and location history to law enforcement. Data used in predictive policing systems.

brokers.name: Fog Riville (Fog Data Science)
brokers.incident: Sells a product called 'Fog Reveal' — a web-based tool allowing law enforcement to search MAID-linked location histories of millions of devices. Marketed as requiring no warrant. Used to build pattern-of-life profiles on individuals by querying their MAID movement history. Named specifically in the Le Monde/French investigation into advertising ID surveillance ecosystems.

brokers.name: Locate X (Babel Street)
brokers.incident: Real-time MAID tracking platform sold exclusively to government and law enforcement. Allows geofence queries — 'show me every MAID that entered this building in the last 30 days.' Used to track visitors to abortion clinics, mosques, and protest sites. Named in Le Monde investigation alongside Fog Riville as commercial MAID surveillance products.
